understanding.goal_understanding: Guide the currently selected piece (P) to a goal cell (+), potentially using other selectable pieces (S) and pushable blocks (B) to clear a path, within the step budget for each level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action attempted to shift the selected piece by one grid cell, which corresponded to a 3-pixel change in the [x, y] position (x right/left, y down/up). If a pushable block was directly adjacent in the move direction and space allowed, it could be pushed along with the player; otherwise movement would be blocked. Walls (#) acted as obstacles that could prevent movement, and some boundaries could behave differently so testing moves mattered; the movement_*_blocked flags were only hints that something was adjacent, not a guaranteed failure. If multiple selectable pieces existed, CLICK with an exact pixel-coordinate target_position (matching the objects list coordinate system) switched control to the selectable at that position.

understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action attempted to shift the selected piece by one grid cell, which corresponded to a 3-pixel change in the [x, y] position (x right/left, y down/up). If a pushable block was directly adjacent in the move direction and space allowed, it could be pushed along with the player; otherwise movement would be blocked.
